content.problemStatement:
You are given NNN segments where each segment is of the form [Li,Ri][L_i,R_i][Li​,Ri​] (1≤Li≤Ri≤N)(1 \leq L_i \leq R_i \leq N)(1≤Li​≤Ri​≤N).

It is ensured that for any two segments XXX and YYY, one of the following conditions hold true:


X∩Y=XX \cap Y = X X∩Y=X,
X∩Y=YX \cap Y = Y X∩Y=Y, or
X∩Y=∅X \cap Y = \emptysetX∩Y=∅ (empty set)
Note that ∩\cap∩ denotes the intersection of segments.

In other words, for any two segments, either one is completely inside the other or they are completely disjoint.

The ithi^{th}ith segment (1≤i≤N)(1\le i \le N)(1≤i≤N) is assigned a value ViV_iVi​.

A subset S={S1,S2,…,Sk}S = \{S_1, S_2, \ldots , S_k\}S={S1​,S2​,…,Sk​} having kkk segments is considered good if there exists an integer array BBB of size kkk such that:


All elements of the array BBB are **distinct**.
BiB_iBi​ lies in the segment SiS_iSi​.
Let the score of a good subset be defined as the sum of values of all segments of the subset. Find the **maximum** score of a good subset.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT - the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains integers NNN - the number of segments.
Each of the next NNN lines contains three integers Li,Ri,L_i,R_i,Li​,Ri​, and ViV_iVi​, describing the ithi^{th}ith segment.


Output Format
For each test case, output the **maximum** score of a good subset.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: A possible value of good subset is S=[A2,A4]S = [A_2, A_4]S=[A2​,A4​]. The array B=[2,3]B = [2, 3]B=[2,3] such that 222 lies in the segment A2A_2A2​ and 333 lies in the segment A4A_4A4​. The sum of values of all segments of SSS is 10+20=3010+20 = 3010+20=30.
It can be proven that the score of a good subset does not exceed 303030 in this case.
Test case 222: We can take S=[A1,A2,A3,A4]S = [A_1, A_2, A_3, A_4]S=[A1​,A2​,A3​,A4​].
The array B=[2,3,1,4]B = [2, 3, 1, 4]B=[2,3,1,4].

For segment A1A_1A1​, L=1L = 1L=1 and R=3R = 3R=3. Here L1≤2≤R1L_1 \le 2 \le R_1L1​≤2≤R1​.
For segment A2A_2A2​, L=2L = 2L=2 and R=3R = 3R=3. Here L2≤3≤R2L_2 \le 3 \le R_2L2​≤3≤R2​.
For segment A3A_3A3​, L=1L = 1L=1 and R=1R = 1R=1. Here L3≤1≤R3L_3 \le 1 \le R_3L3​≤1≤R3​.
For segment A4A_4A4​, L=1L = 1L=1 and R=4R = 4R=4. Here L4≤4≤R4L_4 \le 4 \le R_4L4​≤4≤R4​.

The sum of scores of all segments of SSS is 4+5+10+13=324+5+10+13 = 324+5+10+13=32. It can be proven that the score of a good subset does not exceed 4+5+10+13=324+5+10+13=324+5+10+13=32 in this case.
